preamble: The transformation from 'a' to 'i' represents an extreme case of vowel raising and fronting. It often occurs in unstressed positions where vowels are prone to radical reduction and centralization, eventually settling at the high front corner of the vowel space.

examples.note: Vowel reduction in unstressed open syllables (con- + facio).
Development of vocalic laryngeal/schwa-like sound often results in 'i'.

sources: Weiss, M. (2009). Outline of the Historical and Comparative Grammar of Latin. Beech Stave Press.
